# services.jankyborders

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.jankyborders`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| ---------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `services.jankyborders.active_color` | `string` | Sets the border color for the focused window (format: 0xAARRGGBB). For instance, active_color=“0xff00ff00” creates a green border. For Gradient Border : active_color=“gradient(top_right=0x9992B3F5,bottom_left=0x9992B3F5)” |
| `services.jankyborders.ax_focus` | `boolean` | If set to true, the (slower) accessibility API is used to resolve the focused window. |
| `services.jankyborders.background_color` | `string` | Sets the background fill color for all windows (only 0xAARRGGBB arguments supported). |
| `services.jankyborders.blacklist` | `list of string` | The applications specified here are excluded from being bordered. For example, blacklist = [ “Safari” “kitty” ] excludes Safari and kitty from being bordered. |
| `services.jankyborders.blur_radius` | `floating point number` | Sets the blur radius applied to the borders or backgrounds with transparency. |
| `services.jankyborders.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the jankyborders service… |
| `services.jankyborders.hidpi` | `boolean` | If set to on, the border will be drawn with retina resolution. |
| `services.jankyborders.inactive_color` | `string` | Sets the border color for all windows not in focus (format: 0xAARRGGBB). For Gradient Border : inactive_color=“gradient(top_right=0x9992B3F5,bottom_left=0x9992B3F5)” |
| `services.jankyborders.order` | `one of “above”, “below”` | Specifies whether borders should be drawn above or below windows. |
| `services.jankyborders.package` | `package` | The jankyborders package to use. |
| `services.jankyborders.style` | `string` | Specifies the style of the border (either round or square). |
| `services.jankyborders.whitelist` | `list of string` | Once this list is populated, only applications listed here are considered for receiving a border. If the whitelist is empty (default) it is inactive. |
| `services.jankyborders.width` | `floating point number` | Determines the width of the border. For example, width=5.0 creates a border 5.0 points wide. |
